evidence: Built in Batch 7, generalized in Batch 8, used in Batch 9 and Batch 11, and narrowed by Batch 10.
failureModes: One split only; not a leakage proof; not an official protocol checker by itself.
mustNotUse: Do not use it to claim benchmark reproduction or absence of leakage.
nextUse: Use whenever a benchmark score is reported, paired with dummy, shuffled-label, per-class, seed, and split controls.

evidence: Found useful duplicate/schema evidence in Wine Quality and Dry Bean, but Batch 10 and Batch 11 showed pandas dominates raw single-table findings.
failureModes: Weak semantic inference; needs wrapper context for target columns; raw discoveries are often pandas-equivalent.
mustNotUse: Do not call it a discovery tool on one simple CSV unless it finds something the declared pandas baseline misses.
nextUse: Use for source-hash cards, schema comparison across split files, duplicate/missingness gates, and public evidence packaging.

evidence: Built on pluggy and reused in Batch 8; Batch 10 reaffirmed static inventory as support-only.
failureModes: Static test definitions can diverge from runtime parametrized collection; dependency failures can dominate.
mustNotUse: Do not treat static test inventory as runtime reproduction.
nextUse: Use only beside real pytest collection/execution output.

evidence: Used in Batch 5, Batch 6, Batch 9, and Batch 11 to separate download/provisioning from isolated replay.
failureModes: Requires prepared local data and package availability; cannot validate download-time provenance alone.
mustNotUse: Do not silently fall back to host execution.
nextUse: Use for replay of packaged data, baselines, and custom tools after download/provisioning is complete.

evidence: Batch 6 made ladder depth explicit, and later batches used similar execution/replay/report stages.
failureModes: Can become paperwork if not tied to actual execution.
mustNotUse: Do not count a ladder level without concrete evidence.
nextUse: Use for every deep target to record highest achieved reproduction level and failed steps.

evidence: Batch 11 showed ordinary pandas checks explain most single-table schema findings.
failureModes: Overstates tool value on clean CSVs.
mustNotUse: Do not promote as a raw discovery method.
